message.content: Tool-calling agents need to avoid repeated tool loops to ensure efficient task execution. To prevent this, agents should implement a mechanism to track tool calls, keeping a record of each tool invoked and its result. After executing a tool, agents should check if it has produced a final result or requires further tool calls. If the result is final, agents can proceed to the next task or stop executing additional tools. If more tool calls are needed, agents should verify if the same tool has been used before in the current task context. If so, agents should either stop using the tool or modify the tool call parameters to ensure progress in the task. This approach prevents agents from getting stuck in an endless loop of repeated tool calls and improves overall task efficiency.
